title: neubeckia decora
intro: Neubeckia decora, also known as New Zealand holly, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand and eastern Australia, characterized by its red or purple berries and dark green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with its roots and berries containing compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Neubeckia decora include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Neubeckia decora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or berries in hot water or a solvent, and are often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
